title: Parallelize GitHub Actions tests while keeping AWS steps sequential

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Started work. Goal: reduce CI wall time by parallelizing test execution in GitHub Actions while preserving sequential order for AWS-related stages.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
Implemented workflow parallelization in .github/workflows/ci.yaml:
- Split monolithic test job into python-tests, dashboard-unit-tests, and dashboard-storybook-tests so they run concurrently.
- Kept semantic-release in python-tests to preserve current release gating behavior on push branches.
- Updated trigger-deployment to depend on all three test jobs, so AWS pipeline triggers remain after successful test completion.
- Verified YAML parses successfully (YAML_OK).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Follow-up request: run Python test suite against both Python 3.11 and 3.12 in CI. Implementing matrix strategy while ensuring semantic-release still runs exactly once.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Updated CI to run Python tests on a matrix of Python versions (3.11 and 3.12) in parallel. Added matrix guards so Initialize Version Tag and Semantic Release execute only on the 3.11 leg, preventing duplicate release operations. Workflow YAML validated successfully (YAML_OK).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Follow-up: branch protection/ruleset currently requires old  check. Updating required status checks to new CI job names.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Updated GitHub repository ruleset main-branch-protection (ID: 13251390) required status checks to match new CI jobs: Python Tests (3.11), Python Tests (3.12), Dashboard Unit Tests, Dashboard Storybook Tests. Removed obsolete test context.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Follow-up: eliminate duplicate CI runs by narrowing  trigger to long-lived branches only, since main is protected and staging pushes are rare.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Applying agreed trigger change: run CI on push only for main and staging; keep pull_request trigger for all branches to preserve pre-merge validation.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Updated .github/workflows/ci.yaml triggers: push now limited to main and staging; pull_request remains all branches. This removes duplicate push+PR runs for feature/dev branches while preserving required pre-merge CI checks. YAML validated (YAML_OK).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Emergency rollback to restore CI signal: broadening push trigger again because pull_request checks are not being created for feature/deepgram-input-source (no github-actions suite on latest PR SHA).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Rolled back push trigger filter to ** in .github/workflows/ci.yaml so CI runs on feature branch pushes again. This is an immediate reliability fix while PR-trigger behavior is investigated. YAML validated (YAML_OK).

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Operational cleanup requested: clear Actions backlog by canceling stale queued CodeQL runs and obsolete CI runs, while preserving the newest PR validation run.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Reverting temporary rollback: restricting CI push trigger back to main/staging to avoid duplicate push+PR runs on feature branches.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Temporary push-all rollback removed. CI trigger is now push on main/staging only, plus pull_request on all branches. This prevents duplicate runs on feature/dev branches while keeping PR validation checks.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: GitHub Actions parallelization already implemented. Python tests (with matrix), dashboard unit tests, and storybook tests run in parallel; deployment trigger kept sequential via job dependencies.
